text: I’ll inspect the fixture app’s manifests and test setup first, then audit the dependency graph against current advisory sources and apply the smallest version changes that remove vulnerable packages.
The repository is a large Java fixture with Maven modules under `fixtures/bigapp`. I’m narrowing to dependency declarations and build commands so the remediation stays scoped.

text: `make test` is a Maven install from `fixtures/bigapp/grouper-parent` with tests skipped but test artifacts still built. I’m going to use Maven’s dependency output plus an external advisory scanner if one is available, then confirm any remaining findings manually.

tool:

command -v grype || true

text: There is no local vulnerability scanner installed, so I’m generating the resolved Maven dependency list and will query OSV directly for those Maven coordinates. That avoids relying only on obvious old versions in the parent POM.

The resolved dependency list was produced successfully. I’m parsing unique Maven coordinates now and sending them to OSV in batches so transitive vulnerabilities are included, not just directly declared ones.

tool:
/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' /tmp/bigapp-deps.txt | head -n 260
rg -o \"[A-Za-z0-9_.\\-]+:[A-Za-z0-9_.\\-]+:[A-Za-z0-9_.\\-]+:["'^:[:space:]]+:(compile|runtime|provided|test|system|import)" /tmp/bigapp-deps.txt | sed '"'s/:jar:/:/' | sort -u > /tmp/bigapp-coords.txt
wc -l /tmp/bigapp-coords.txt
sed -n '1,80p' /tmp/bigapp-coords.txt"

text: I have 252 unique resolved coordinates. Some are duplicated at different versions because different modules pull different transitive paths, so I’ll treat each package/version pair independently and then remediate at the version-management points.

tool:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import json, urllib.request
coords=[]
for line in open('/tmp/bigapp-coords.txt'):
    parts=line.strip().split(':')
    if len(parts) < 4: continue
    group, art, ver = parts[0], parts[1], parts[2]
    # skip local snapshots
    if ver.endswith('SNAPSHOT'): continue
    coords.append((group,art,ver))
# de-dupe package/version
seen=[]; s=set()
for c in coords:
    k=c
    if k not in s:
        s.add(k); seen.append(c)
results=[]
for i in range(0,len(seen),100):
    batch=seen[i:i+100]
    payload={\"queries\":[{\"package\":{\"ecosystem\":\"Maven\",\"name\":f\"{g}:{a}\"},\"version\":v} for g,a,v in batch]}
    req=urllib.request.Request('https://api.osv.dev/v1/querybatch', data=json.dumps(payload).encode(), headers={'Content-Type':'application/json'})
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=60) as r:
        data=json.load(r)
    for (g,a,v), res in zip(batch, data.get('results', [])):
        vulns=res.get('vulns') or []
        if vulns:
            results.append({'package':f'{g}:{a}', 'version':v, 'vulns':[{'id':x.get('id'), 'summary':x.get('summary'), 'aliases':x.get('aliases'), 'modified':x.get('modified')} for x in vulns]})
print(json.dumps(results, indent=2))
print('COUNT', len(results))
PY"

text: OSV found vulnerable resolved runtime/compile packages, including several transitives from older SDKs and web-service stacks. I’m going to rerun the query across all scopes as a check, then inspect dependency paths for each vulnerable coordinate so the fixes target the source declarations instead of adding broad overrides blindly.

tool:
/bin/bash -lc "mvn -B -DskipTests dependency:list -DappendOutput=true -DoutputFile=/tmp/bigapp-deps-all.txt >/tmp/mvn-deplist-all.log && rg -o \"[A-Za-z0-9_.\\-]+:[A-Za-z0-9_.\\-]+:([A-Za-z0-9_.\\-]+:)?["'^:[:space:]]+:(compile|runtime|provided|test|system|import)" /tmp/bigapp-deps-all.txt | sort -u > /tmp/bigapp-coords-all-raw.txt && python3 - <<'"'PY'
import re
out=[]
for line in open('/tmp/bigapp-coords-all-raw.txt'):
    p=line.strip().split(':')
    if len(p)==5: g,a,typ,v,scope=p
    elif len(p)==6: g,a,typ,clas,v,scope=p
    else: continue
    out.append(f'{g}:{a}:{v}:{scope}')
open('/tmp/bigapp-coords-all.txt','w').write('\\n'.join(sorted(set(out)))+'\\n')
print(len(set(out)))
PY"

text: The all-scope list has 419 unique package/version/scope entries, so there are test-only and plugin-adjacent libraries beyond the runtime list. I’m checking those too before editing.

import json, urllib.request
coords=[]
for line in open('/tmp/bigapp-coords-all.txt'):
    p=line.strip().split(':')
    if len(p)<4: continue
    g,a,v=p[0],p[1],p[2]
    if v.endswith('SNAPSHOT'): continue
    coords.append((g,a,v))
seen=[]; s=set()
for c in coords:

results=[]
for i in range(0,len(seen),100):

    payload={\"queries\":[{\"package\":{\"ecosystem\":\"Maven\",\"name\":f\"{g}:{a}\"},\"version\":v} for g,a,v in batch]}

PY"

text: The all-scope scan matches the runtime findings: 21 vulnerable package/version entries. Next I’m fetching fixed-version ranges and Maven paths, because a few of these may be unfixable at their old coordinates or require excluding a transitive dependency.

Most findings have clear fixed versions; two are special cases: `commons-httpclient` and `commons-lang` are end-of-life coordinates with no fixed release in OSV, so they need removal/replacement rather than a simple version bump. I’m mapping which modules still pull them in.

The vulnerable dependencies are concentrated in the parent POM, the Grouper API/UI modules, the WS modules, and a few provisioner modules. I’m reading those POM sections now and will edit only version management, explicit replacements, and necessary exclusions.

text: For Bouncy Castle, the vulnerable coordinate is the retired `*-jdk15on` line. Since that line has no non-vulnerable release after 1.70, I’m testing whether the build works with those transitives excluded and the maintained `*-jdk18on` artifacts already used elsewhere.

`commons-lang:commons-lang` is used directly throughout the codebase under the old `org.apache.commons.lang` package, and OSV has no fixed version for that coordinate. I’ll document that explicitly, but I’m still checking whether the vulnerable resolved artifact can be eliminated from dependency resolution without a broad source migration.
The first source search hit ripgrep’s default regex limitation; I’m rerunning it with PCRE2 and then I’ll make the version/exclusion edits.

Hibernate’s advisory lists 5.6.15 as last affected rather than giving a fixed event. I’m checking whether a 5.6.x fixed artifact exists before deciding whether a major-version migration would be required.

text: `hibernate-core` has no available 5.6.16 artifact, so this fixture cannot remove that CVE with a patch-level version change. I’m applying the available fixed updates and central overrides now, then I’ll rescan to see what remains and document unfixable items explicitly.

I’m making these remediation edits: upgrade centrally managed vulnerable libraries, move Axis2 usages to 1.8.2, override vulnerable transitives like Netty/Guava/OAuth/Neethi/MIME4J, replace retired Bouncy Castle `jdk15on` usage with the maintained `jdk18on` line, and leave `commons-lang`/Hibernate for documented no-fixed-version handling after verification.
The parent POM now carries the fixed versions and transitive overrides. I’m updating the module POMs that pin vulnerable versions outside parent management.

The first edit pass is in. I’m rebuilding the resolved dependency list now; if Maven rejects any upgraded web-service dependency or a vulnerable transitive remains, I’ll adjust from the actual resolution rather than guessing.
Maven accepted the edited dependency graph. I’m running the same OSV batch check against the post-edit resolution now.

text: `commons-httpclient` is only used in the installer module, not across the whole app. Since it is a small surface and has no fixed 3.x release, I’m replacing that usage with the already-managed Apache HttpClient 4 dependency instead of leaving it unresolved.

I found one unused helper tied to the old Commons HttpClient API; removing it lets the installer drop that vulnerable dependency cleanly. I’m also adding the remaining fixed overrides and excluding Bouncy Castle `jdk15on` from the Box SDK.
The combined patch missed one utility-method context, so I’m applying the same changes in smaller patches.
Now I’m changing the installer’s small download helper to HttpClient 4 and dropping the unused Commons HttpClient helper.
One unused method remains in a CRLF-formatted file; I’m removing just that method block and preserving the rest of the file.
The HttpClient 3 dependency and its only source references are removed. I’m rerunning resolution and OSV again to confirm the graph.
Resolution still succeeds after the second edit pass. I’m checking the final vulnerable set now.

text: The only OSV findings left after remediation are migration/no-fixed-version cases: Jackson databind’s advisory has no fixed event for the `com.fasterxml.jackson.core` 2.x coordinate, Commons Lang 2.x has no fixed release, and Hibernate’s 5.6 line has no available fixed artifact under the existing coordinate. I’m moving Jackson to the latest available 2.18 patch anyway, then I’ll run the root test target.
I’m running `make test` from the repository root now. This is the authoritative build check for the fixture.

`make test` exposed an API break from Qpid JMS 2.x: it moved from `javax.jms` to `jakarta.jms`. I’m backing that down to the newest 1.x line that keeps `javax.jms`, while retaining explicit Netty overrides for the Netty CVEs.

text: The final OSV scan reports only three unresolved packages, all documented as no fixed compatible version in this coordinate line. I’m adding `FIXES.md` now with every package found and the action taken.
`FIXES.md` is written. I’m doing a quick final status/diff review so the summary is accurate and no generated build artifacts were accidentally included.

Implemented the dependency remediation and recorded it in [FIXES.md](/tmp/bomly-study-824zbawy/codex-bare-bigapp-3-1783754249/repo/FIXES.md).

Key changes:
- Updated vulnerable Maven versions and transitive overrides in the parent/module POMs.
- Replaced retired `commons-httpclient` usage in the installer with Apache HttpClient 4.
- Excluded retired Bouncy Castle `jdk15on` artifacts and used maintained `jdk18on` artifacts.
- Verified final OSV scan: only `jackson-databind`, `commons-lang`, and `hibernate-core` remain, each documented in `FIXES.md` as having no compatible fixed version/coordinate for this app line.

Verification:
- `make test` passes at the repo root.
